perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall mean any and all non -public information, including, without limitation, technical, developmental, market ing, sales, operating, performance, cost, know -how, business plans, business methods, and process information, disclosed to the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall mean any information, including, without limitation, technical, developmental, market ing, sales, operating, performance, cost, know -how, business plans, business methods, and process information, disclosed to the Recipient. The Disclosing party reserves the right to not consider certain information as confidential.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction by initially defining 'Confidential Information' broadly as 'any information,' but then adding that the Disclosing Party can selectively choose what isn't considered confidential. This contradicts the initial broad definition and creates uncertainty about what information is actually protected under the NDA. This is particularly problematic because it undermines the purpose of the entire agreement, as participants may not know what information is truly covered, creating legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed to the Recipient will be used solely for the Business Purpose and for no other purpose whatsoever. The Recipient agrees to keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidentia l Information confidential and to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Information with the same degree of care with which it protects the confidentiality of its own confidential information, but in no event with less than a reasonable degree o f care. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors on a need -to-know basis, and only if such employees, agents, consultants and contractors have executed appropriate written agreements with Re cipient sufficient to enable Recipient to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement. Recipient shall not make any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information except as needed for the Business Purpose. At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipi ent shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by adding a caveat that the disclosing party can choose what isn't confidential. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes all 'Confidential Information' disclosed is subject to specific protections and usage restrictions. The change creates uncertainty about what information is actually protected, thus creating an in-text contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors on a need -to-know basis, and only if such employees, agents, consultants and contractors have executed appropriate written agreements with Re cipient sufficient to enable Recipient to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to anyone they deem fit, with no prior consent needed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text limits disclosure to those with a 'need-to-know' and requires written agreements. The modified text allows unrestricted disclosure, contradicting the confidentiality obligation. This undermines the core purpose of the NDA and creates a direct conflict, making it uncertain whether the recipient has any obligation to keep information confidential or not. This is directly contradicting with point 2 of the same file, where it is mentioned that the Recipient needs to keep the Disclosing Party's Confidential information safe.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed to the Recipient will be used solely for the Business Purpose and for no other purpose whatsoever. The Recipient agrees to keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidentia l Information confidential and to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Information with the same degree of care with which it protects the confidentiality of its own confidential information, but in no event with less than a reasonable degree o f care. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to anyone they deem fit, with no prior consent needed. Recipient shall not make any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information except as needed for the Business Purpose. At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipi ent shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, "Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to anyone they deem fit, with no prior consent needed," directly contradicts the contradicted text, "The Recipient agrees to keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidentia l Information confidential". This is a strong in-text contradiction because it fundamentally alters the confidentiality obligations of the recipient, creating a clear conflict and uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the confidentiality clause. This adheres to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All right title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain with Disclosing Par ty or its licensors.
perturbation.changed_text: All right title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall transfer to the Recipient upon disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states ownership remains with the Disclosing Party. The modified text transfers ownership to the Recipient, creating a direct contradiction. This creates significant uncertainty about who owns the confidential information and what rights each party has. This contradiction impacts any clause regarding the use, reproduction, or disposal of the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall transfer to the Recipient upon disclosure. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WAR RANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON -INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that ownership of Confidential Information remains with the Disclosing Party. The changed text explicitly transfers the ownership to the Recipient. The 'Contradicted Text' repeats the changed text's assertion that ownership transfers to the Recipient, but the 'Perturbation Explanation' indicates this transfer creates a contradiction. Therefore, a direct contradiction exists because the ownership is being claimed to be with Disclosing Party originally, but the Changed and Contradicted text is transferring ownership to the Recipient, creating an inconsistency regarding ownership of the Confidential Information as described in the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations and limitations set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall not apply to information which is: (a) at any time in the public domain, other than by a breach on the part of the Recipient; or (b) at any time rightfully received from a third party which had the right to and transmits it to the Recipient without any obligation of confidentialit y.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations and limitations set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall not apply to any information whatsoever. This agreement applies only for moral purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text lists specific exceptions to confidentiality. The modified text eliminates all obligations and limitations. stating the agreement is only for 'moral purposes,' which is non-enforceable. This nullifies the entire purpose of the NDA by rendering the confidentiality provisions meaningless, directly contradicting all other sections of the agreement that outline confidentiality obligations and remedies for breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, or in the event that a breach appears to be imminent, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to all legal and equitable remedies afforded it by law, and in addition may recover all reaso nable costs and attorneys' fees incurred in seeking such remedies. If the Confidential Information is sought by any third party, including by way of subpoena or other court process, the Recipient shall inform the Disclosing Party of the request in suffici ent time to permit the Disclosing Party to object to and, if necessary, seek court intervention to prevent the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the obligations and limitations regarding Confidential Information do not apply and that the agreement is only for moral purposes. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines remedies for breach of the agreement and the Recipient's obligations regarding third-party requests for Confidential Information, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The original text limited the scope of the Confidential Information definition to certain items, where the Changed Text completely removes the Confidentiality clause altogether.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, or in the event that a breach appears to be imminent, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to all legal and equitable remedies afforded it by law, and in addition may recover all reaso nable costs and attorneys' fees incurred in seeking such remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall only be entitled to a written apology.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides legal and equitable remedies for breach. The modified text limits the remedy to a written apology, creating a direct contradiction in the enforcement mechanism. This makes it unclear what recourse the Disclosing Party has if the Recipient violates the NDA, significantly weakening the agreement's enforceability and creating legal ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall only be entitled to a written apology. If the Confidential Information is sought by any third party, including by way of subpoena or other court process, the Recipient shall inform the Disclosing Party of the request in suffici ent time to permit the Disclosing Party to object to and, if necessary, seek court intervention to prevent the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for legal and equitable remedies upon breach of the agreement. The changed text limits the remedy to only a written apology. The contradicted text repeats the change, further reinforcing the contradiction. This creates a direct conflict regarding the remedies available to the Disclosing Party, making the contract's enforceability unclear as per the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The validity, construction and enforceability of this Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the law of the state of Colorado .
perturbation.changed_text: The validity, construction and enforceability of this Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the law of the state of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by changing the governing law of the agreement from Colorado to Delaware. This is an in-text contradiction because all other clauses assume the agreement is interpreted under Colorado law. Legal interpretations and precedents vary by state, so changing the governing law fundamentally alters the meaning and enforceability of all provisions. This creates significant uncertainty about which state's laws apply, leading to potential disputes over the agreement's interpretation and validity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The validity, construction and enforceability of this Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the law of the state of Delaware. This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective Parties. This Agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement between the Parties with respect to the sub ject matter hereof. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as o f the date first above written. Pitch Deck F ire confirms that this information has been made cl ear and publically available to all event attendees. Pitch Deck Fire agrees that if a Participant delivers to Pitch Deck Fire any information or data marked or identified as confidential or proprietary ("confidential information"), then Pitch Deck Fire shall not, except as otherwise permitted or instructed by the applicable Participant in writing (a) disclose or otherwise make available the confidential information to any third party except to the extent otherwise expressly permitted by this Agreement, (b) modify, copy, transmit, alter, merge, decompile, dis assemble, reverse engineer or adapt any portion of the confidential information. Name: Stacie Shaw__________ Title: Founder and Lead Designer – Pitch Deck Fire __ Signature : ____________ _____________ ______ Date: 12/1/2016 ____
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement is governed by Colorado law, while the changed text modifies this to Delaware law. The contradicted text also states that the agreement is governed by Delaware law, which on the surface looks like no contradiction. However, because the original text specified Colorado and this was later modified to Delaware (and a justification was given that the other clauses in the document assume Colorado law to be valid), and there is a section elsewhere in the contract that says the governing law is Delaware, this means that the entire contract now needs to be interpreted under Delaware law. The original contract was meant to be interpreted under Colorado law. This change therefore creates a direct conflict with the original assumption that the entire agreement was initially predicated on the interpretation of the agreement under Colorado law. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, as it impacts the validity and enforceability of the entire agreement.
